title: AI Product Data Ingestion Pipeline
description: Build the ingestion layer for an AI product: parse raw inputs, validate structure, version outputs, and make them reusable for evals, retrieval, or downstream services.
whyItMatters: Frontier AI products live or die by their data path. This project shows you can build the rails that keep product systems grounded and maintainable.
prerequisites: Complete the Python for AI Engineers lesson

steps.title: Define the pipeline requirements
steps.description: Choose a realistic scenario: merging customer data from a CRM export (CSV), transaction data from an API (JSON), and product data from a database dump (CSV). Define what the clean output should look like.

steps.title: Build the extraction layer
steps.description: Write functions that load data from each source format. Handle encoding issues, malformed rows, and missing files gracefully.
steps.tips: Always log what you loaded: 'Loaded 10,432 rows from transactions.json, 3 rows had parsing errors'.

steps.title: Build the transformation layer
steps.description: Clean the data: handle missing values (fill, drop, or flag), standardise formats (dates, currencies, phone numbers), remove duplicates, and merge datasets on common keys.

steps.title: Add data validation
steps.description: Write validation checks: no null values in required fields, all dates within expected range, all prices positive, referential integrity between tables. Log violations.

steps.title: Build the load layer
steps.description: Write the clean, merged dataset to Parquet format (columnar, compressed, typed). Also output a data quality report: row counts, null percentages, validation results.

steps.title: Make it rerunnable
steps.description: Structure the pipeline so it can run end-to-end with a single command. Add a CLI using argparse or typer with options for input/output paths and verbosity.
steps.tips: A good pipeline is idempotent — running it twice on the same input produces the same output.

deliverables: Python ETL pipeline with extract, transform, and load stages
Clean output dataset in Parquet format
CLI interface for running the pipeline

rubric.description: All three stages (extract, transform, load) work end-to-end from raw sources to clean output.
Validation checks catch real data quality issues and produce a clear report.
The pipeline handles malformed data, missing files, and edge cases without crashing.
Running the pipeline twice on the same input produces identical output. The CLI is well-documented.
